nodes.context: Philadelphia-based African-American organized crime syndicate active from 1960s to early 1980s, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and other rackets.
Italian-American Mafia family in Philadelphia, led by Angelo Bruno, involved in traditional organized crime.
New York-based Mafia family that did business with the Black Mafia.
Religious organization; many Black Mafia members converted to Islam, giving the group the nickname 'Muslim Mafia'.
Successor or splinter group of the Black Mafia, mentioned at the end of the text.
Italian-American Mafia; used as a model by the JBM and had links with JBM members.
Philadelphia-based Italian-American Mafia family; cooperated with JBM in cocaine distribution.
Jamaican drug gang; rival of the JBM in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.
Allied with the Black Mafia according to the infobox.

edges.context: Italian organized crime controlled east of Broad Street, Black Mafia west of Broad Street, with a tacit understanding.
Gambino family did business with the Black Mafia, supplying drugs through Frank Matthews.
Many Black Mafia members converted to Islam and became Nation of Islam members, leading to the nickname 'Muslim Mafia'.
Junior Black Mafia is mentioned as a later iteration or splinter of the original Black Mafia.
JBM was formed by former Black Mafia members and received mentorship from them.
JBM modeled itself after LCN and had links with LCN members for drug supply.
JBM cooperated with the Bruno-Scarfo family in cocaine distribution.
JBM was involved in a drug war with the Shower Posse in Southwest Philadelphia.
Many Black Mafia members were also Nation of Islam members.
The Council is listed as an ally of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
The Philadelphia crime family is listed as an ally of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
Shower Posse is listed as a rival of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
